mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides a straightforward way to bootstrap a secure and minimal viable cluster, making it ideal for users who want to set up clusters quickly without extensive configuration. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of certain aspects and the requirement for a compatible environment.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

```

> Add Google Cloud public key for apt.
> Add Kubernetes apt repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
> Prevent automatic upgrades of these packages.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This step initializes the Kubernetes control plane on the master node.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory.
> Copy the admin config file.
> Change ownership of the config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the control plane node is in 'Ready' state.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes all Kubernetes components and configurations from the node. Warning: This will result in data loss.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove kubeconfig directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration, drain the node, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node status before upgrading.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that the node is back to 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet service may not be started or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' and check the status with 'sudo systemctl status kubelet'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources or incompatible OS settings.

**Fix:**
Ensure the node meets the minimum requirements and check the logs for specific errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod network not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The network add-on may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the network add-on and ensure it is compatible with your Kubernetes version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have joined the cluster properly.

**Fix:**
Check the kubelet logs and ensure the node is correctly configured to join the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new API versions and improved upgrade procedures.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain flags related to network configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new API changes and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing your control plane node in 'Ready' state.

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
